claim: The hyperbranched sulfur networks provide a thermal resistance above 200 °C.
rationale: Abstract states a high thermal resistance (>220 °C); >220 entails >200, subject+unit co-located, no hedge/scope. Happy-path anchor — must stay ACCEPT on every commit.

claim: The effective work function for aluminum-polyimide is 1.7 eV.
rationale: Abstract: 'effective work function for aluminum-polyimide is estimated to be 1.7 eV in the temperature range'. Value is verbatim present; this row guards the physical-measurement condition suffix fix.

claim: The conductivity measurements were carried out at 30 °C.
rationale: CrossRef has no abstract; OpenAlex provides a DOI-bound abstract with 'Measurements were carried out at 30°C ... in the range'. This row guards OpenAlex reachability plus physical range/field condition handling.

claim: The sulfur networks were synthesized on a 200 g scale.
rationale: Abstract: 'we synthesized a 200 g scale of amorphous, ... hyperbranched polymeric sulfur networks'. This row guards subject binding across descriptive comma clauses.

claim: Actuated strains up to 117% were demonstrated with silicone elastomers.
rationale: Abstract verbatim: 'Actuated strains up to 117% were demonstrated with silicone elastomers'. This row guards claim-side 'up to' comparator handling.

claim: This paper reports a dielectric breakdown strength of 1200 MV/m.
rationale: Fabricated number absent from the abstract. Negative control — must never ACCEPT.

claim: The AC conductivity follows sigma_ac proportional to omega^s with the exponent s approaching 1.
rationale: Relational claim, no value+unit; not explicitly stated in the abstract. Out of scope — must never ACCEPT.

claim: The isothermal current decays as i(t) proportional to 1/t for a uniform trap distribution.
rationale: Relational. OpenAlex may make the abstract reachable, but reachability must not turn an out-of-scope relational claim into ACCEPT.

claim: Kapton HN deep trap energies are in the range 0.79 to 1.05 eV.
rationale: Elsevier withholds the abstract from CrossRef and OpenAlex; the 0.79-1.05 eV deep-trap values live in full text, not any abstract. Verdict may be UNVERIFIABLE (no abstract) or PARTIAL (Semantic Scholar abstract reached but the values are not in it) depending on S2 availability; both satisfy the must_not_accept invariant. Abstract-only ceiling — must never ACCEPT.

claim: The dielectric response exponent n lies between 0 and 1.
rationale: No abstract in any source (old Nature) and the claim is relational. Must remain UNVERIFIABLE.

claim: This material shows 95% energy conversion efficiency.
rationale: DOI does not resolve. Must fail/UNVERIFIABLE, never ACCEPT.

claim: The polymeric sulfur networks provide a high thermal resistance of 220 °C.
rationale: Abstract evidence is '(>220 °C)'. An exact '220 °C' claim must NOT be entailed by '>220'; this row guards against exact-claim over-acceptance.
